theory.intro: You can estimate an angle's size by comparing it with familiar **benchmark** angles before measuring.
facts: Key benchmarks: $90^{\circ}$ (right angle), $180^{\circ}$ (straight), $45^{\circ}$ (half a right angle), $360^{\circ}$ (full turn).
Smaller than the square corner $\Rightarrow$ less than $90^{\circ}$; wider $\Rightarrow$ more.
A good estimate is usually within about $10^{\circ}$ of the true value.
steps: Compare the angle with the nearest benchmark.
Decide if it is a bit more or a bit less.
